opp.name: Nippon Life Insurance Company_ Opp - April '26
opp.next_step: Tailored demo focused only on iSource, iSupplier, iContract, and Autonomous Negotiation Agent in a Nippon-like workflow Deep dive on AI especially how negotiation agent works with real scenarios and controls Share use cases relevant to insurance or services firms with similar scale Map current process → future state (light workshop style) Include procurement + IT stakeholder for integration confidence
opp.ais_why:
• SF description captures direct prospect-side ANA interest: Nippon is exploring how AI, particularly the Autonomous Negotiation Agent, can enhance cost savings and consistency in supplier negotiations.
• Customer is asking for an ANA-specific deep dive with real scenarios and controls — the strongest AI-pull signal short of a transcript.
• No Avoma calls tied to this opp yet despite recent activity, so confidence stays Medium because evidence is SF-only.

items.quote: Zero Avoma buyer calls discovered via opp+account+attendee email pulls; no transcripts, no notes, no voice-of-customer captured.
No Economic Buyer identified; single Decision Maker contact role (Derrick Staat, Global Supply Chain Director) with no budget-approval authority mapped.
No champion identified or developing; Derrick Staat shows no advocacy evidence and deal has been silent for 30 days.
No business problem, savings target, ROI case, or quantified pain documented.
No evaluation scorecard, RFP requirements, or buyer-stated must-haves documented.
Buyer's vendor selection steps, approval chain, and timeline gates unknown.
No competitors named; unclear if Nippon Life is running competitive RFP, evaluating incumbents, or sole-sourcing.
Only 1 buyer-side contact role on opportunity (Derrick Staat); no IT, finance, COO, or executive sponsor linked.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, savings target, cost reduction, cycle-time improvement, or ROI case is documented in Salesforce or captured on any Avoma call. The opportunity description and all custom business-problem fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c) returned no data. Zero buyer calls were discovered, so no qualitative pain articulation exists either. The Next Step references 'Map current process to future state (light workshop style)', which implies the discovery of Metrics is planned but not yet executed. Until that workshop runs and the buyer names their procurement/contract pain and quantifies impact (headcount, cycle time, error rate, spend under management), Metrics remain a complete gap. Recommended move: run the tailored demo and process-mapping workshop to extract and quantify the buyer's procurement pain, then document it.

champion.narrative: No champion is identified or developing. The single contact role, Derrick Staat (Global Supply Chain Director, Decision Maker), is a functional buyer but shows no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or active engagement. Zero Avoma calls exist to demonstrate that Derrick has introduced other stakeholders, shared internal docs, pushed back on competitors, or driven the Zycus agenda. The deal has been silent for 30 days (last activity 2026-05-27), and the planned demo/workshop sequence has not executed, which suggests Derrick is not actively championing the deal internally. The Salesforce field X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is empty, and no Champion contact role exists. No other buyer-side contacts (IT, finance, COO) are linked to the opportunity. Recommended move: restart engagement with Derrick Staat, execute the tailored demo and process workshop, assess his willingness to advocate internally, and ask him to introduce a senior executive sponsor (CFO or COO) who can champion the deal at the budget-approval level; if Derrick cannot or will not, find another path into the organization or reassess deal viability.

competition.narrative: Competition is completely unknown. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty (reported as 'unknown' in ground truth). The synthesis-map fields (Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c) returned no data. Zero Avoma calls exist to capture competitor names or buyer benchmarking statements. The Next Step does not reference any competitive positioning. With no discovery calls, no RFI/RFP activity documented, and no field intel, it is unknown whether Nippon Life is running a competitive process, sole-sourcing Zycus, evaluating incumbents (e.g. SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua), or comparing to a do-nothing/manual baseline. Recommended move: during the tailored demo, ask the buyer directly how they are addressing procurement/contract challenges today, whether they are evaluating other vendors, and if so, who; document the competitive landscape and tailor the Merlin/ANA negotiation-agent story as the wedge if peer SaaS vendors are in play.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain is documented. The Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are all empty. Zero Avoma calls exist to capture the buyer's articulated pain. The Next Step references 'Map current process to future state (light workshop style)', which suggests pain discovery is planned (the workshop would uncover current-state friction), but it has not yet executed. The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, iContract, ANA negotiation agent) implies procurement and contract-management pain, but without the buyer stating it, this is inferred, not confirmed. No pain owner is named. Recommended move: run the process-mapping workshop and ask the buyer to describe their current procurement/contract pain (manual workflows, cycle time, supplier negotiation leverage, contract visibility, compliance risk) and quantify impact; capture verbatim quotes and identify the pain owner (likely Derrick Staat or a COO-level sponsor).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal redline process, InfoSec review, procurement channel, SI/partner involvement) are completely unknown. Zero Avoma calls and zero Salesforce documentation exist. The Next Step mentions 'Include procurement + IT stakeholder for integration confidence', which implies InfoSec and systems-integration scoping is planned but not begun. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function is recorded. No partner or SI is named. Recommended move: when the tailored demo advances to commercials, ask the buyer to describe their contracting workflow (legal review cycle, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement approval, ERP/systems integration requirements) and add the IT stakeholder to the conversation early to de-risk the paper process.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified. The single contact role is Derrick Staat (Global Supply Chain Director, Decision Maker role), a functional procurement lead but not the budget authority for a $100k SaaS deal at an enterprise insurer. The Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all empty. Zero Avoma calls exist to reveal who controls commercial conversations or pricing. The account-level contact roster includes C-level executives (Warren Veltman, President and CEO; Thomas DeByle, SVP and CFO; David Acselrod, EVP and COO; Francine Young, EVP and COO), but none are linked to this opportunity via contact role, task, or call. The Next Step instructs 'Include procurement + IT stakeholder for integration confidence' but does not name an Economic Buyer or executive sponsor. Recommended move: during the tailored demo, ask Derrick Staat who must approve the budget and pricing, then secure an introduction to that executive (likely CFO or COO) and add them as a contact role with Economic Buyer designation.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process (vendor selection steps, approval chain, timeline gates) is unknown. The Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are empty. Zero Avoma calls exist to map how Nippon Life runs vendor evaluations. The single contact role (Derrick Staat, Decision Maker) suggests he owns the functional procurement selection, but with no Economic Buyer, champion, or other influencers mapped, the approval chain is invisible. The deal is 63 days old and still in Qualified (no progression to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted), with no field-history stage movements, which may signal a slow enterprise motion or a stalled process. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty (no renewal forcing function). Recommended move: during the next engagement, ask Derrick Staat to walk through Nippon Life's vendor selection process (RFI, RFP, shortlist, demos, reference checks, approvals), the timeline for each gate, and who must sign off at each step; document it and map the committee.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal decision criteria, RFP requirements, or evaluation scorecard are documented. The Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c returned no data. Zero Avoma calls exist to capture use-case validation, test-case sessions, or buyer-stated must-haves. The Next Step outlines what Zycus plans to show ('Tailored demo focused only on iSource, iSupplier, iContract, and Autonomous Negotiation Agent'; 'Deep dive on AI especially how negotiation agent works with real scenarios and controls'), which implies the criteria discovery is forward-looking, not complete. The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, iContract, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) is broad, but without knowing which capabilities are must-haves versus nice-to-haves, or how Nippon Life will score vendors, the criteria remain unknown. Recommended move: during the tailored demo and process-mapping workshop, ask the buyer to articulate their top 3-5 evaluation criteria and any weighted scorecard; document it in Business_Requirements__c and confirm whether AI/Merlin negotiation agent is a formal requirement or a differentiation lever.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null, and zero buyer calls exist to capture whether Nippon Life has articulated AI appetite, agentic-automation interest, or negotiation-agent use cases. The product scope includes Merlin, Agentic AI, and ANA (Autonomous Negotiation Agent), and the Next Step plans an AI deep-dive, which suggests Zycus is leading with AI, but buyer receptivity is unvalidated. Without discovery calls or a recorded AIS assessment, the tier is unconfirmed.

Next Step was updated 2026-06-05 with a tailored-demo plan but has not executed. No buyer calls, no stage movements, no task completions in 90-day window. The deal is live per LastActivityDate but stalled on the planned demo/workshop sequence.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole, Avoma: 0 calls, LastActivityDate 2026-05-27 (30 days ago)
champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Derrick Staat (Global Supply Chain Director, Decision Maker) is the single buyer-side contact but shows no advocacy, internal selling, or active engagement. The deal has been silent for 30 days, the planned tailored demo and workshop have not executed, and no other stakeholders are mapped. Derrick has not introduced IT, finance, or executive sponsors, has not shared internal docs, and has not driven the Zycus agenda forward. Without a champion, the deal is at high risk of stalling indefinitely or being displaced by a competitor with stronger internal advocacy.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal shows cooling signals: 30-day silence since last activity (2026-05-27), planned tailored demo and workshop unexecuted for 21 days, zero buyer calls on record, single-threaded contact (Derrick Staat) with no multi-threading into IT or finance, no Economic Buyer or champion, and no forward stage progression in 63 days. The Next Step was updated 2026-06-05 (21 days ago) but has not translated into action, suggesting either buyer disengagement or internal Zycus execution delay. Confidence is low.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-04-24 (63 days ago), qualified immediately; 151 days to close (2026-11-24). Last activity 2026-05-27 (30 days ago). Time in Qualified stage: at least 63 days (submission dates unavailable). No Avoma buyer calls discovered via opp+account+attendee pulls. Next step planned (tailored demo + AI deep-dive + use-case sharing + process workshop + IT stakeholder inclusion) is unexecuted for 30 days. No forward movement since late May.
evidence: Stage Qualified with 151 days to close allows ample runway, but zero buyer engagement on record (no calls) and 30-day stall on the planned demo/workshop sequence undermines Pipeline forecast.
Single Decision Maker contact role (Derrick Staat, Global Supply Chain Director) with no Economic Buyer, no champion, no multi-threading, and no recent two-way dialogue.
Product scope is ambitious (iSource, iSupplier, iContract, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) but no evidence of discovery depth, pain quantification, or buyer-articulated business case.
No Tasks, Events, field history moves, or Avoma calls to corroborate active deal work since 2026-05-27.
Recommend Omitted until the demo executes and buyer engagement resumes.
north_star_verdict.headline: EARLY-stage qualified deal stalled 30 days with zero buyer calls on record, single-threaded through one Decision Maker, no Economic Buyer or champion mapped, and planned tailored demo unexecuted since late May; competitive landscape unknown.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with strong AI product scope but zero discovery depth and 30-day execution stall. Critical gaps: no buyer calls, no Economic Buyer, no champion, single-threaded, no quantified pain, no competitive intel. Immediate actions: restart engagement, execute the overdue tailored demo/workshop sequence, and multi-thread into IT and finance stakeholders.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown. Zero competitors are named in Salesforce fields, Avoma calls, Next Steps, or Tasks. It is unclear whether Nippon Life is running a competitive RFP, evaluating incumbents, or sole-sourcing Zycus. Without discovery calls or buyer dialogue, the competitive landscape is invisible. Recommended action: ask the buyer during the tailored demo how they are solving procurement/contract challenges today and whether other vendors are being evaluated.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI product scope is strong and differentiated (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA negotiation agent are in Products field), and the Next Step explicitly calls for 'Deep dive on AI especially how negotiation agent works with real scenarios and controls', which shows deal-team intent to lead with AI. However, zero buyer calls and zero discovery mean the AI story has not yet landed with Nippon Life, and buyer appetite for AI/Merlin is unconfirmed. The AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are all null, so no AI hunger signal exists. Under-positioned risk is high: the AI narrative is planned but undelivered.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer fit is unvalidated. The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, iContract, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA negotiation agent) is ambitious and AI-forward, but zero buyer calls and zero documented discovery mean no use-case validation, fit confirmation, or buyer-stated criteria exist. The Next Step outlines what Zycus plans to demonstrate (tailored demo, AI deep-dive, use-case sharing, process mapping), but this is forward-looking, not completed. Fit remains theoretical until the demo executes and the buyer confirms alignment.

value.flag: Execute the 21-day-overdue tailored demo and process-mapping workshop immediately to restart momentum, validate AI/Merlin fit, extract quantified procurement pain, and assess Derrick Staat's internal advocacy and willingness to champion the deal (if he cannot or will not, this is a disqualify signal).

value.flag: Multi-thread beyond Derrick Staat by asking him to include the IT stakeholder (Amy Lee, Enterprise Systems Manager, or Puneet Karnawat, VP IT) and a finance/COO executive (Thomas DeByle, CFO, or Francine Young, COO) in the next demo/workshop; map the Economic Buyer and decision committee before advancing to commercials.

value.flag: Discover the competitive landscape during the tailored demo by asking how Nippon Life is addressing procurement/contract challenges today, whether other vendors are being evaluated, and if so, who; position Merlin/ANA negotiation agent as the wedge if peer SaaS competitors emerge.

gaps: Zero Avoma buyer calls discovered via opp (006P700000XDJ9t 15-char) + account (0012000000mbCegAAE) + attendee email (derrick.staat@nninc.com, d-acselrod@nipponlifebenefits.com, and other account-level contact emails) pulls; no transcripts, no notes, no voice-of-customer.
No Economic Buyer identified; single Decision Maker contact role (Derrick Staat) with no budget-approval authority mapped.
No champion identified or developing; Derrick Staat shows no advocacy evidence.
No quantified business problem, savings target, or ROI case documented.
No decision criteria, evaluation scorecard, or buyer-stated must-haves captured.
No decision process or approval chain mapped.
Competition unknown; no competitors named in any source.
AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null; buyer AI appetite unconfirmed.
Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c all unavailable; true time-in-stage cannot be computed from submission dates.
Only 1 buyer-side contact role; below multi-thread bar (hygiene gap, but signals weak relationship breadth).
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Formal Evaluation')
